title: stauntonia obovatifoliola subsp. urophylla
intro: Stauntonia obovatifoliola subsp. urophylla is a plant species in the family Annonaceae, also known as the custard apple or soursop family. It is a climbing vine with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant contains compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may contribute to its health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. Traditionally, Stauntonia obovatifoliola subsp. urophylla is prepared as a decoction or infusion, often used in herbal medicine to promote well-being and alleviate symptoms of various ailments.
